services.title: Interior House Painting
services.description: Full interior painting with premium prep, professional-grade paints, and a 2-year warranty.
services.intro: A professional interior paint job is 80% prep and 20% paint. We fill every hole, sand every rough edge, tape every surface, and apply premium paint — so the finish looks right from day one and holds up for years.
features: Full furniture moving and room protection with drop cloths
Patching and sanding of nail holes, cracks, and imperfections
Caulking of trim gaps and baseboards for a clean line
Premium primer on all bare surfaces and stain-blocking where needed
Two full coats of Sherwin-Williams or Benjamin Moore paint
Crisp cut-in lines at all trim, ceilings, and transitions
Final touch-up walkthrough before leaving
2-year workmanship warranty on all interior work

process.title: Free Estimate & Color Consult
process.desc: We measure, discuss your color vision, and give you a written quote — often same week.

process.title: Prep Day
process.desc: We protect your floors and furniture, fill imperfections, caulk gaps, and prime problem areas.

process.title: Painting
process.desc: Two full coats applied in the correct order — walls, ceilings, trim — for a professional finish.

process.title: Walkthrough & Touch-Up
process.desc: We walk every room with you and address any touch-ups before we call the job complete.

faqs.q: How long does interior painting take?
faqs.a: A single room is typically done in one day. A full home of 1,500–2,500 sq ft usually takes 2–4 days depending on the number of rooms and prep needed.

faqs.q: Do I need to move my furniture?
faqs.a: We move most furniture as part of the job. We ask that you clear personal items and valuables from shelves and surfaces before we arrive.

faqs.q: What paint brands do you use?
faqs.a: We use Sherwin-Williams Emerald and Benjamin Moore Aura as our standard — both are low-VOC, washable, and self-priming in most applications. We don't cut costs with builder-grade paints.

services.title: Exterior House Painting
services.description: Complete exterior paint job — pressure wash, prep, prime, and two coats for lasting curb appeal.
services.intro: Your home's exterior takes a beating from sun, rain, and temperature swings. A quality exterior paint job starts with thorough surface prep — pressure washing, scraping, sanding, and priming — before a single drop of paint goes on.
features: Full pressure washing of all painted surfaces
Scraping and feathering of peeling paint
Caulking of all siding gaps, trim joints, and penetrations
Spot priming on bare wood and stain-blocking on problem areas
Two full coats of exterior paint on all siding
Separate coat on all trim, fascia, soffits, and shutters
Masking and protection of windows, doors, and landscaping
5-year exterior workmanship warranty

process.title: Free Estimate
process.desc: We walk the exterior, note all prep needs, and give you a detailed written quote with paint specs.

process.title: Prep & Prime
process.desc: We power wash, scrape, caulk, and prime — the most important step for a lasting exterior finish.

process.title: Two-Coat Application
process.desc: Siding is painted first, then all trim. We work methodically to avoid laps and ensure full coverage.

process.title: Final Inspection
process.desc: We walk the entire exterior with you and touch up anything that doesn't meet our standard.

faqs.q: How long does exterior painting last?
faqs.a: A properly prepped and painted exterior with a premium product lasts 7–10 years. Our 5-year warranty covers any peeling or adhesion failures.

faqs.q: What weather conditions are needed for exterior painting?
faqs.a: We paint when temperatures are between 50°F and 90°F and no rain is forecast for 24 hours. We schedule around weather and reschedule at no charge for rain delays.

faqs.q: Do I need to be home during the exterior paint job?
faqs.a: Not necessarily. We just need access to your water spigot and a way to confirm the color. Most customers drop off a key or leave us a code.

services.title: Cabinet Painting & Refinishing
services.description: Factory-smooth cabinet finishes that transform your kitchen for a fraction of replacement cost.
services.intro: Cabinet painting is the highest-ROI kitchen upgrade available. At 20–30% of the cost of new cabinets, you get a fresh color, a durable factory-like finish, and zero construction dust. We spray every door off-site for a smooth, brush-mark-free result.
features: All doors and drawer fronts removed and sprayed off-site
Full degreasing and scuff sanding of all surfaces
Bonding primer on all cabinet surfaces
Spray application for brush-mark-free finish
2–3 coats of cabinet-specific enamel or urethane
Box painting in-place with professional brushwork
New hinges installed if upgrading hardware
Durable finish rated for moisture and kitchen use

process.title: Free Cabinet Assessment
process.desc: We evaluate your cabinet material, condition, and layout to confirm they're good candidates for painting.

process.title: Prep & Prime
process.desc: Doors go to our shop. Boxes are degreased, sanded, and primed in your kitchen with containment.

process.title: Spray Finish
process.desc: Doors receive 2–3 spray coats for a factory-smooth finish. Box interiors and frames are rolled and brushed.

process.title: Reinstall & Adjust
process.desc: We rehang every door, adjust for level and alignment, and clean up completely.

faqs.q: How long do painted cabinets last?
faqs.a: With proper prep and a quality enamel or urethane topcoat, painted cabinets hold up well for 5–8 years with normal use before any touch-up is needed.

faqs.q: Can all cabinet types be painted?
faqs.a: Solid wood and MDF cabinets paint beautifully. Thermofoil and laminate cabinets can be painted but require specific bonding primers. Particle board with peeling laminate is generally not a good candidate.

faqs.q: Will I be able to use my kitchen during the project?
faqs.a: We try to maintain sink and appliance access throughout. Most projects take 3–5 days. We'll walk through the schedule at your estimate.

services.title: Deck & Fence Staining
services.description: Deck and fence staining and sealing that protects wood from UV, moisture, and foot traffic.
services.intro: An unprotected wood deck grays, cracks, and rots within a few years. A properly cleaned and stained deck looks great and lasts twice as long. We use professional-grade stains that penetrate the wood fiber rather than sitting on top.
features: Full deck pressure washing and brightening
Sanding of rough or splintered boards
Wood brightener applied to open grain and remove tannins
Semi-transparent or solid stain application
Two coats on horizontal surfaces for maximum protection
Separate treatment of railings, balusters, and stairs
Fence cleaning and staining or painting
3-year warranty on deck staining work

process.title: Assessment & Quote
process.desc: We inspect the wood condition, identify any rotten boards needing replacement, and quote the full scope.

process.title: Clean & Prep
process.desc: We pressure wash, apply brightener, and allow proper dry time before any stain is applied.

process.title: Stain Application
process.desc: We apply two coats of penetrating stain to decking surfaces and one coat to vertical elements.

process.title: Dry & Inspect
process.desc: We do a final inspection after the stain cures and address any thin spots or missed areas.

faqs.q: How often should I restain my deck?
faqs.a: A quality semi-transparent stain on a well-prepped deck lasts 2–4 years on horizontal surfaces. Solid stains last longer but are harder to renew. We'll recommend the right product for your situation.

faqs.q: Can you stain a deck that was previously painted?
faqs.a: Paint must be fully stripped before stain is applied. If previous coatings are intact, we can restain with a solid-body product. We'll assess at the estimate.

faqs.q: What's the difference between paint and stain for a deck?
faqs.a: Stain penetrates wood fibers and allows moisture to escape, reducing peeling. Paint sits on the surface and is more likely to peel over time on horizontal surfaces exposed to weather.

services.title: Popcorn Ceiling Removal
services.description: Remove outdated popcorn texture and leave a smooth, modern ceiling — ready to paint.
services.intro: Popcorn ceilings trap dust, look dated, and can contain asbestos in homes built before 1979. We remove the texture, skim-coat a smooth finish, and prime — leaving you with a fresh ceiling ready for paint.
features: Asbestos test kit review or referral before work begins
Full room protection with plastic sheeting and drop cloths
Water-spraying and scraping of all texture
Skim coat applied for a perfectly smooth finish
Sanding and priming of new surface
Paint-ready finish in 1–2 days per room
Furniture moving and protection included
Complete cleanup — no texture dust left behind

process.title: Asbestos Confirmation
process.desc: For homes built before 1979, we confirm the ceiling has been tested negative before any scraping begins.

process.title: Room Protection
process.desc: We fully plastic off the room — walls, floors, furniture — before wetting and scraping the texture.

process.title: Skim Coat & Sand
process.desc: After scraping, we apply a thin skim coat of joint compound, sand smooth, and repeat until the surface is flat.

process.title: Prime & Paint
process.desc: We prime the new surface and apply your chosen ceiling paint for a clean, finished result.

faqs.a: Popcorn texture applied before 1979 may contain asbestos. We require a negative asbestos test before any scraping. We can refer you to a certified tester if needed.

faqs.q: Can I paint over popcorn ceiling instead of removing it?
faqs.a: Yes, painting is cheaper and easier — but it doesn't remove the dated look. If you want a smooth, modern ceiling, removal and skim coating is the only option.

faqs.q: How long does it take per room?
faqs.a: A typical bedroom takes 1 day for scraping and skim coat, plus 1 day for sanding, priming, and painting — 2 days total per room. We can often do 2–3 rooms simultaneously.

services.title: Commercial Painting
services.description: Interior and exterior commercial painting for offices, retail, and multi-unit properties.
services.intro: Commercial painting projects demand speed, minimal disruption, and professional results. We work nights, weekends, and phased schedules to keep your business open — and our crew is bonded, drug-tested, and OSHA-trained.
features: Night and weekend scheduling to minimize business disruption
OSHA-trained and safety-certified crew
Bonded and fully insured for commercial properties
Airless spray application for large surfaces
Epoxy floor coatings for warehouses and garages
Parking lot line striping and stenciling
Multi-unit and apartment complex programs
Commercial-grade low-VOC products for occupied spaces

process.title: Walkthrough & Scope
process.desc: We assess the full project, note surface conditions, and develop a work schedule around your business hours.

process.title: Proposal & Timeline
process.desc: You receive a detailed written proposal with start date, phase schedule, and completion target.

process.title: Phased Execution
process.desc: We work in phases or during off-hours to keep your space operational throughout the project.

process.title: Final Sign-Off
process.desc: We walk every area with your facility manager and address any items before invoicing.

faqs.q: Can you work without disrupting our business operations?
faqs.a: Yes. We routinely schedule nights, weekends, and phased work to keep businesses open. We'll build a custom schedule around your operating hours.
Yes. All crew members are background-checked, bonded, and covered under our commercial general liability policy. We can provide certificates of insurance with your property listed.

faqs.q: Do you handle multi-unit apartment communities?
faqs.a: Yes. We have ongoing relationships with property management companies and can handle unit turns, common areas, and full building exteriors on a scheduled program.

howItWorks.h2: Four steps to a freshly painted home.

steps.title: Free Estimate
steps.desc: We measure your space, discuss colors, and give you a detailed written quote — often the same week.

steps.title: Professional Prep
steps.desc: We protect your home, fill all imperfections, and prime before any paint goes on.

steps.title: Flawless Paint
steps.desc: Two coats of premium paint applied by experienced painters — clean lines, full coverage, no shortcuts.

steps.title: Walkthrough & Warranty
steps.desc: We inspect every surface with you and back the job with our 2-year workmanship warranty.

projects.title: Exterior Paint Job
projects.material: Benjamin Moore Hale Navy with White Trim

serviceOptions: Not Sure — I Need an Assessment

items.q: What paint brands do you use?
items.a: We use Sherwin-Williams Emerald, Duration, and SuperPaint, and Benjamin Moore Aura and Advance as our primary product lines. These are professional-grade paints with better coverage, washability, and durability than big-box store brands.

items.q: Do I need to move furniture before the painters arrive?
items.a: We move most furniture as part of the job. We ask that you remove personal items, valuables, and wall décor before we arrive. Large furniture that can't be moved will be masked and covered.

items.q: How long does it take to paint a whole house interior?
items.a: A typical 1,500–2,000 sq ft home with 3–4 bedrooms takes 2–4 days for a full interior repaint. Homes with more prep needs or detailed trim work may take longer.
Yes. Our standard products — Sherwin-Williams Emerald and Benjamin Moore Aura — are zero or low-VOC. We can stay on the zero-VOC selection if you have sensitivities.
We provide a 2-year workmanship warranty on interior painting and a 5-year warranty on exterior painting. If anything peels or fails due to application, we fix it at no cost.

cta.h2: Your home deserves a finish it can be proud of.
cta.subtext: Get a free estimate from a professional painting crew — flat-rate pricing, premium paints, and a 2-year warranty on every job.
